href="http://snowball.tartarus.org/">http://snowball.tartarus.org/</a></p><p>The KStem stemmer in</p><p>analysis/common/src/org/apache/lucene/analysis/en</p><p>was developed by Bob Krovetz and Sergio Guzman-Lara (CIIR-UMass Amherst)</p><p>under the BSD-license.</p><p>The Arabic,Persian,Romanian,Bulgarian, Hindi and Bengali analyzers (common) come with a default</p><p>stopword list that is BSD-licensed created by Jacques Savoy. These files reside in:</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ar/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fa/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ro/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/bg/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/hi/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/bn/stopwords.txt</p><p>See <a href="http://members.unine.ch/jacques.savoy/clef/index.html">http://members.unine.ch/jacques.savoy/clef/index.html</a>.</p><p>The German,Spanish,Finnish,French,Hungarian,Italian,Portuguese,Russian and Swedish light stemmers</p><p>(common) are based on BSD-licensed reference implementations created by Jacques Savoy and</p><p>Ljiljana Dolamic. These files reside in:</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/de/GermanLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/de/GermanMinimalStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/es/SpanishLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fi/FinnishLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fr/FrenchLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fr/FrenchMinimalStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/hu/HungarianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/it/ItalianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/pt/PortugueseLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ru/RussianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/sv/SwedishLightStemmer.java</p><p>The Stempel analyzer (stempel) includes BSD-licensed software developed</p><p>by the Egothor project <a href="http://egothor.sf.net/">http://egothor.sf.net/</a>, created by Leo Galambos, Martin Kvapil,</p><p>and Edmond Nolan.</p><p>The Polish analyzer (stempel) comes with a default</p><p>stopword list that is BSD-licensed created by the Carrot2 project. The file resides</p><p>in stempel/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/pl/stopwords.txt.</p><p>See <a href="http://project.carrot2.org/license.html">http://project.carrot2.org/license.html</a>.</p><p>The SmartChineseAnalyzer source code (smartcn) was</p><p>provided by Xiaoping Gao and copyright 2009 by <a href="http://www.imdict.net">www.imdict.net</a>.</p><p>WordBreakTestUnicode_*.java (under modules/analysis/common/src/test/)</p><p>is derived from Unicode data such as the Unicode Character Database.</p><p>See <a href="http://unicode.org/copyright.html">http://unicode.org/copyright.html</a> for more details.</p><p>The Morfologik analyzer (morfologik) includes BSD-licensed software</p><p>developed by Dawid Weiss and Marcin Miłkowski (<a href="http://morfologik.blogspot.com/">http://morfologik.blogspot.com/</a>).</p><p>Morfologik uses data from Polish ispell/myspell dictionary</p><p>(<a href="http://www.sjp.pl/slownik/en/">http://www.sjp.pl/slownik/en/</a>) licenced on the terms of (inter alia)</p><p>LGPL and Creative Commons ShareAlike.</p><p>Morfologic includes data from BSD-licensed dictionary of Polish (SGJP)</p><p>(<a href="http://sgjp.pl/morfeusz/">http://sgjp.pl/morfeusz/</a>)</p><p>Servlet-api.jar and javax.servlet-*.jar are under the CDDL license, the original</p><p>source code for this can be found at <a href="http://www.eclipse.org/jetty/downloads.php">http://www.eclipse.org/jetty/downloads.php</a></p><p>===========================================================================</p><p>Kuromoji Japanese Morphological Analyzer - These files reside in:</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/de/GermanLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/de/GermanMinimalStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/es/SpanishLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fi/FinnishLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fr/FrenchLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fr/FrenchMinimalStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/hu/HungarianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/it/ItalianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/pt/PortugueseLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ru/RussianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/sv/SwedishLightStemmer.java</p><p>The Stempel analyzer (stempel) includes BSD-licensed software developed</p><p>by the Egothor project <a href="http://egothor.sf.net/">http://egothor.sf.net/</a>, created by Leo Galambos, Martin Kvapil,</p><p>and Edmond Nolan.</p><p>The Polish analyzer (stempel) comes with a default</p><p>stopword list that is BSD-licensed created by the Carrot2 project. See <a href="http://www.brics.dk/automaton/">http://www.brics.dk/automaton/</a></p><p>The levenshtein automata tables (under core/src/java/org/apache/lucene/util/automaton) were automatically generated with the moman/finenight FSA library, created by Jean-Philippe Barrette-LaPierre. This library is available under an MIT license, see <a href="http://sites.google.com/site/rrettesite/moman">http://sites.google.com/site/rrettesite/moman</a> and <a href="http://bitbucket.org/jpbarrette/moman/overview/">http://bitbucket.org/jpbarrette/moman/overview/</a></p><p>The class org.apache.lucene.util.WeakIdentityMap was derived from the Apache CXF project and is Apache License 2.0.</p><p>The class org.apache.lucene.util.compress.LZ4 is a Java rewrite of the LZ4 compression library (<a href="https://github.com/lz4/lz4/tree/dev/lib">https://github.com/lz4/lz4/tree/dev/lib</a>) that is licensed under the 2-clause BSD license.</p><p>(<a href="https://opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php">https://opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php</a>)</p><p>The Google Code Prettify is Apache License 2.0. See <a href="http://code.google.com/p/google-code-prettify/">http://code.google.com/p/google-code-prettify/</a></p><p>This product includes code (JaspellTernarySearchTrie) from Java Spelling Checking Package (jaspell): <a href="http://jaspell.sourceforge.net/License">http://jaspell.sourceforge.net/License</a>: The BSD License (<a href="http://www.opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php">http://www.opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php</a>)</p><p>The snowball stemmers in analysis/common/src/java/net/sf/snowball were developed by Martin Porter and Richard Boulton.</p><p>The snowball stopword lists in analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/snowball were developed by Martin Porter and Richard Boulton.</p><p>The full snowball package is available from <a href="https://snowballstem.org/">https://snowballstem.org/</a></p><p>The KStem stemmer in analysis/common/src/org/apache/lucene/analysis/en was developed by Bob Krovetz and Sergio Guzman-Lara (CIIR-UMass Amherst) under the BSD-license.</p><p>The Arabic,Persian,Romanian,Bulgarian, Hindi and Bengali analyzers (common) come with a default</p><p>stopword list that is BSD-licensed created by Jacques Savoy. These files reside in:</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ar/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fa/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ro/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/bg/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/hi/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/bn/stopwords.txt</p><p>See <a href="http://members.unine.ch/jacques.savoy/clef/index.html">http://members.unine.ch/jacques.savoy/clef/index.html</a>.</p><p>The German,Spanish,Finnish,French,Hungarian,Italian,Portuguese,Russian and Swedish light stemmers (common) are based on BSD-licensed reference implementations created by Jacques Savoy and Ljiljana Dolamic. These files reside in:</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/de/GermanLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/de/GermanMinimalStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/es/SpanishLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fi/FinnishLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fr/FrenchLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fr/FrenchMinimalStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/hu/HungarianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/it/ItalianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/pt/PortugueseLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ru/RussianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/sv/SwedishLightStemmer.java</p><p>The Stempel analyzer (stempel) includes BSD-licensed software developed by the Egothor project <a href="http://egothor.sf.net/">http://egothor.sf.net/</a>, created by Leo Galambos, Martin Kvapil, and Edmond Nolan.</p><p>The Polish analyzer (stempel) comes with a default stopword list that is BSD-licensed created by the Carrot2 project. The file resides in stempel/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/pl/stopwords.txt. See <a href="https://github.com/carrot2/carrot2">https://github.com/carrot2/carrot2</a>.</p><p>The SmartChineseAnalyzer source code (smartcn) was provided by Xiaoping Gao and copyright 2009 by <a href="http://www.imdict.net">www.imdict.net</a>.</p><p>WordBreakTestUnicode_*.java (under modules/analysis/common/src/test/) is derived from Unicode data such as the Unicode Character Database. See <a href="http://unicode.org/copyright.html">http://unicode.org/copyright.html</a> for more details.</p><p>The Morfologik analyzer (morfologik) includes BSD-licensed software developed by Dawid Weiss and Marcin Miłkowski (<a href="https://github.com/morfologik/morfologik-stemming">https://github.com/morfologik/morfologik-stemming</a>) and uses data from the BSD-licensed dictionary of Polish (SGJP, <a href="http://sgjp.pl/morfeusz/">http://sgjp.pl/morfeusz/</a>).</p><p>Servlet-api.jar and javax.servlet-*.jar are under the CDDL license, the original source code for this can be found at <a href="http://www.eclipse.org/jetty/downloads.php">http://www.eclipse.org/jetty/downloads.php</a></p><p>===========================================================================</p><p>Kuromoji Japanese Morphological Analyzer - These files reside in:</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ar/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fa/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ro/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/bg/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/hi/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/bn/stopwords.txt</p><p>See <a href="http://members.unine.ch/jacques.savoy/clef/index.html">http://members.unine.ch/jacques.savoy/clef/index.html</a>.</p><p>The German,Spanish,Finnish,French,Hungarian,Italian,Portuguese,Russian and Swedish light stemmers</p><p>(common) are based on BSD-licensed reference implementations created by Jacques Savoy and</p><p>Ljiljana Dolamic. These files reside in:</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/de/GermanLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/de/GermanMinimalStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/es/SpanishLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fi/FinnishLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fr/FrenchLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fr/FrenchMinimalStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/hu/HungarianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/it/ItalianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/pt/PortugueseLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ru/RussianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/sv/SwedishLightStemmer.java</p><p>The Stempel analyzer (stempel) includes BSD-licensed software developed</p><p>by the Egothor project <a href="http://egothor.sf.net/">http://egothor.sf.net/</a>, created by Leo Galambos, Martin Kvapil,</p><p>and Edmond Nolan.</p><p>The Polish analyzer (stempel) comes with a default</p><p>stopword list that is BSD-licensed created by the Carrot2 project. The file resides</p><p>in stempel/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/pl/stopwords.txt.</p><p>See <a href="https://project.carrot2.org/license.html">https://project.carrot2.org/license.html</a>.</p><p>The SmartChineseAnalyzer source code (smartcn) was</p><p>provided by Xiaoping Gao and copyright 2009 by <a href="http://www.imdict.net">www.imdict.net</a>.</p><p>WordBreakTestUnicode_*.java (under modules/analysis/common/src/test/)</p><p>is derived from Unicode data such as the Unicode Character Database.</p><p>See <a href="http://unicode.org/copyright.html">http://unicode.org/copyright.html</a> for more details.</p><p>The Morfologik analyzer (morfologik) includes BSD-licensed software</p><p>developed by Dawid Weiss and Marcin Miłkowski (<a href="http://morfologik.blogspot.com/">http://morfologik.blogspot.com/</a>).</p><p>Morfologik uses data from Polish ispell/myspell dictionary</p><p>(<a href="http://www.sjp.pl/slownik/en/">http://www.sjp.pl/slownik/en/</a>) licenced on the terms of (inter alia)</p><p>LGPL and Creative Commons ShareAlike.</p><p>Morfologic includes data from BSD-licensed dictionary of Polish (SGJP)</p><p>(<a href="http://sgjp.pl/morfeusz/">http://sgjp.pl/morfeusz/</a>)</p><p>Servlet-api.jar and javax.servlet-*.jar are under the CDDL license, the original</p><p>source code for this can be found at <a href="http://www.eclipse.org/jetty/downloads.php">http://www.eclipse.org/jetty/downloads.php</a></p><p>===========================================================================</p><p>Kuromoji Japanese Morphological Analyzer - Apache Lucene Integration</p><p>===========================================================================</p><p>This software includes a binary and/or source version of data from</p><p>mecab-ipadic-2.7.0-20070801</p><p>which can be obtained from</p><p><a href="http://atilika.com/releases/mecab-ipadic/mecab-ipadic-2.7.0-20070801.tar.gz">http://atilika.com/releases/mecab-ipadic/mecab-ipadic-2.7.0-20070801.tar.gz</a></p><p>or</p><p><a href="http://jaist.dl.sourceforge.net/project/mecab/mecab-ipadic/2.7.0-20070801/mecab-ipadic-2.7.0-20070801.tar.gz">http://jaist.dl.sourceforge.net/project/mecab/mecab-ipadic/2.7.0-20070801/mecab-ipadic-2.7.0-20070801.tar.gz</a></p><p>===========================================================================</p><p>mecab-ipadic-2.7.0-20070801 Notice</p><p>===========================================================================</p><p>Nara Institute of Science and Technology (NAIST),</p><p>the copyright holders, disclaims all warranties with regard to this</p><p>software, including all implied warranties of merchantability and</p><p>fitness, in no event shall NAIST be liable for</p><p>any special, indirect or consequential damages or any damages</p><p>whatsoever resulting from loss of use, data or profits, whether in an</p><p>action of contract, negligence or other tortuous action, arising out</p><p>of or in connection with the use or performance of this software.</p><p>A large portion of the dictionary entries</p><p>originate from ICOT Free Software. project and is Apache License 2.0.</p><p>The class org.apache.lucene.util.compress.LZ4 is a Java rewrite of the LZ4</p><p>compression library (<a href="https://github.com/lz4/lz4/tree/dev/lib">https://github.com/lz4/lz4/tree/dev/lib</a>) that is licensed</p><p>under the 2-clause BSD license.</p><p>(<a href="https://opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php">https://opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php</a>)</p><p>The Google Code Prettify is Apache License 2.0.</p><p>See <a href="http://code.google.com/p/google-code-prettify/">http://code.google.com/p/google-code-prettify/</a></p><p>This product includes code (JaspellTernarySearchTrie) from Java Spelling Checkin</p><p>g Package (jaspell): <a href="http://jaspell.sourceforge.net/">http://jaspell.sourceforge.net/</a></p><p>License: The BSD License (<a href="http://www.opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php">http://www.opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php</a>)</p><p>The snowball stemmers in</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/net/sf/snowball</p><p>were developed by Martin Porter and Richard Boulton.</p><p>The snowball stopword lists in</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/snowball</p><p>were developed by Martin Porter and Richard Boulton.</p><p>The full snowball package is available from</p><p><a href="https://snowballstem.org/">https://snowballstem.org/</a></p><p>The KStem stemmer in</p><p>analysis/common/src/org/apache/lucene/analysis/en</p><p>was developed by Bob Krovetz and Sergio Guzman-Lara (CIIR-UMass Amherst)</p><p>under the BSD-license.</p><p>The Arabic,Persian,Romanian,Bulgarian, Hindi and Bengali analyzers (common) come with a default</p><p>stopword list that is BSD-licensed created by Jacques Savoy. These files reside in:</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ar/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fa/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ro/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/bg/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/hi/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/bn/stopwords.txt</p><p>See <a href="http://members.unine.ch/jacques.savoy/clef/index.html">http://members.unine.ch/jacques.savoy/clef/index.html</a>.</p><p>The German,Spanish,Finnish,French,Hungarian,Italian,Portuguese,Russian and Swedish light stemmers</p><p>(common) are based on BSD-licensed reference implementations created by Jacques Savoy and</p><p>Ljiljana Dolamic. These files reside in:</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/de/GermanLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/de/GermanMinimalStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/es/SpanishLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fi/FinnishLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fr/FrenchLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fr/FrenchMinimalStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/hu/HungarianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/it/ItalianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/pt/PortugueseLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ru/RussianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/sv/SwedishLightStemmer.java</p><p>The Stempel analyzer (stempel) includes BSD-licensed software developed</p><p>by the Egothor project <a href="http://egothor.sf.net/">http://egothor.sf.net/</a>, created by Leo Galambos, Martin Kvapil,</p><p>and Edmond Nolan.</p><p>The Polish analyzer (stempel) comes with a default</p><p>stopword list that is BSD-licensed created by the Carrot2 project. The file resides</p><p>in stempel/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/pl/stopwords.txt.</p><p>See <a href="https://github.com/carrot2/carrot2">https://github.com/carrot2/carrot2</a>.</p><p>The SmartChineseAnalyzer source code (smartcn) was</p><p>provided by Xiaoping Gao and copyright 2009 by <a href="http://www.imdict.net">www.imdict.net</a>.</p><p>WordBreakTestUnicode_*.java (under modules/analysis/common/src/test/)</p><p>is derived from Unicode data such as the Unicode Character Database.</p><p>See <a href="http://unicode.org/copyright.html">http://unicode.org/copyright.html</a> for more details.</p><p>The Morfologik analyzer (morfologik) includes BSD-licensed software</p><p>developed by Dawid Weiss and Marcin Miłkowski</p><p>(<a href="https://github.com/morfologik/morfologik-stemming">https://github.com/morfologik/morfologik-stemming</a>) and uses</p><p>data from the BSD-licensed dictionary of Polish (SGJP, <a href="http://sgjp.pl/morfeusz/">http://sgjp.pl/morfeusz/</a>).</p><p>Servlet-api.jar and javax.servlet-*.jar are under the CDDL license, the original</p><p>source code for this can be found at <a href="http://www.eclipse.org/jetty/downloads.php">http://www.eclipse.org/jetty/downloads.php</a></p><p>===========================================================================</p><p>Kuromoji Japanese Morphological Analyzer - Apache Lucene Integration</p><p>===========================================================================</p><p>This software includes a binary and/or source version of data from</p><p>mecab-ipadic-2.7.0-20070801</p><p>which can be obtained from</p><p><a href="http://atilika.com/releases/mecab-ipadic/mecab-ipadic-2.7.0-20070801.tar.gz">http://atilika.com/releases/mecab-ipadic/mecab-ipadic-2.7.0-20070801.tar.gz</a></p><p>or</p><p><a href="http://jaist.dl.sourceforge.net/project/mecab/mecab-ipadic/2.7.0-20070801/mecab-ipadic-2.7.0-20070801.tar.gz">http://jaist.dl.sourceforge.net/project/mecab/mecab-ipadic/2.7.0-20070801/mecab-ipadic-2.7.0-20070801.tar.gz</a></p><p>===========================================================================</p><p>mecab-ipadic-2.7.0-20070801 Notice</p><p>===========================================================================</p><p>Nara Institute of Science and Technology (NAIST),</p><p>the copyright holders, disclaims all warranties with regard to this</p><p>software, including all implied warranties of merchantability and</p><p>fitness, in no event shall NAIST be liable for</p><p>any special, indirect or consequential damages or any damages</p><p>whatsoever resulting from loss of use, data or profits, whether in an</p><p>action of contract, negligence or other tortuous action, arising out</p><p>of or in connection with the use or performance of this software.</p><p>A large portion of the dictionary entries</p><p>originate from ICOT Free Software. href="http://junit.sourceforge.net/cpl-v10.html">http://junit.sourceforge.net/cpl-v10.html</a></p><p>This product includes code (JaspellTernarySearchTrie) from Java Spelling Checkin</p><p>g Package (jaspell): <a href="http://jaspell.sourceforge.net/">http://jaspell.sourceforge.net/</a></p><p>License: The BSD License (<a href="http://www.opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php">http://www.opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php</a>)</p><p>The snowball stemmers in</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/net/sf/snowball</p><p>were developed by Martin Porter and Richard Boulton.</p><p>The snowball stopword lists in</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/snowball</p><p>were developed by Martin Porter and Richard Boulton.</p><p>The full snowball package is available from</p><p><a href="https://snowballstem.org/">https://snowballstem.org/</a></p><p>The KStem stemmer in</p><p>analysis/common/src/org/apache/lucene/analysis/en</p><p>was developed by Bob Krovetz and Sergio Guzman-Lara (CIIR-UMass Amherst)</p><p>under the BSD-license.</p><p>The Arabic,Persian,Romanian,Bulgarian, Hindi and Bengali analyzers (common) come with a default</p><p>stopword list that is BSD-licensed created by Jacques Savoy. These files reside in:</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ar/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fa/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ro/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/bg/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/hi/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/bn/stopwords.txt</p><p>See <a href="http://members.unine.ch/jacques.savoy/clef/index.html">http://members.unine.ch/jacques.savoy/clef/index.html</a>.</p><p>The German,Spanish,Finnish,French,Hungarian,Italian,Portuguese,Russian and Swedish light stemmers</p><p>(common) are based on BSD-licensed reference implementations created by Jacques Savoy and</p><p>Ljiljana Dolamic. These files reside in:</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/de/GermanLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/de/GermanMinimalStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/es/SpanishLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fi/FinnishLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fr/FrenchLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fr/FrenchMinimalStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/hu/HungarianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/it/ItalianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/pt/PortugueseLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ru/RussianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/sv/SwedishLightStemmer.java</p><p>The Stempel analyzer (stempel) includes BSD-licensed software developed</p><p>by the Egothor project <a href="http://egothor.sf.net/">http://egothor.sf.net/</a>, created by Leo Galambos, Martin Kvapil,</p><p>and Edmond Nolan.</p><p>The Polish analyzer (stempel) comes with a default</p><p>stopword list that is BSD-licensed created by the Carrot2 project. The file resides</p><p>in stempel/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/pl/stopwords.txt.</p><p>See <a href="https://project.carrot2.org/license.html">https://project.carrot2.org/license.html</a>.</p><p>The SmartChineseAnalyzer source code (smartcn) was</p><p>provided by Xiaoping Gao and copyright 2009 by <a href="http://www.imdict.net">www.imdict.net</a>.</p><p>WordBreakTestUnicode_*.java (under modules/analysis/common/src/test/)</p><p>is derived from Unicode data such as the Unicode Character Database.</p><p>See <a href="http://unicode.org/copyright.html">http://unicode.org/copyright.html</a> for more details.</p><p>The Morfologik analyzer (morfologik) includes BSD-licensed software</p><p>developed by Dawid Weiss and Marcin Miłkowski (<a href="http://morfologik.blogspot.com/">http://morfologik.blogspot.com/</a>).</p><p>Morfologik uses data from Polish ispell/myspell dictionary</p><p>(<a href="http://www.sjp.pl/slownik/en/">http://www.sjp.pl/slownik/en/</a>) licenced on the terms of (inter alia)</p><p>LGPL and Creative Commons ShareAlike.</p><p>Morfologic includes data from BSD-licensed dictionary of Polish (SGJP)</p><p>(<a href="http://sgjp.pl/morfeusz/">http://sgjp.pl/morfeusz/</a>)</p><p>Servlet-api.jar and javax.servlet-*.jar are under the CDDL license, the original</p><p>source code for this can be found at <a href="http://www.eclipse.org/jetty/downloads.php">http://www.eclipse.org/jetty/downloads.php</a></p><p>===========================================================================</p><p>Kuromoji Japanese Morphological Analyzer - See <a href="http://unicode.org/copyright.html">http://unicode.org/copyright.html</a> for more details.</p><p>Brics Automaton (under core/src/java/org/apache/lucene/util/automaton) is BSD-licensed, created by Anders Møller. See <a href="http://www.brics.dk/automaton/">http://www.brics.dk/automaton/</a></p><p>The levenshtein automata tables (under core/src/java/org/apache/lucene/util/automaton) were automatically generated with the moman/finenight FSA library, created by Jean-Philippe Barrette-LaPierre. This library is available under an MIT license, see <a href="http://sites.google.com/site/rrettesite/moman">http://sites.google.com/site/rrettesite/moman</a> and <a href="http://bitbucket.org/jpbarrette/moman/overview/">http://bitbucket.org/jpbarrette/moman/overview/</a></p><p>The class org.apache.lucene.util.WeakIdentityMap was derived from the Apache CXF project and is Apache License 2.0.</p><p>The class org.apache.lucene.util.compress.LZ4 is a Java rewrite of the LZ4 compression library (<a href="https://github.com/lz4/lz4/tree/dev/lib">https://github.com/lz4/lz4/tree/dev/lib</a>) that is licensed under the 2-clause BSD license. (<a href="https://opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php">https://opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php</a>)</p><p>The Google Code Prettify is Apache License 2.0.</p><p>See <a href="http://code.google.com/p/google-code-prettify/">http://code.google.com/p/google-code-prettify/</a></p><p>JUnit (junit-4.10) is licensed under the Common Public License v. 1.0</p><p>See <a href="http://junit.sourceforge.net/cpl-v10.html">http://junit.sourceforge.net/cpl-v10.html</a></p><p>This product includes code (JaspellTernarySearchTrie) from Java Spelling Checkin g Package (jaspell): <a href="http://jaspell.sourceforge.net/">http://jaspell.sourceforge.net/</a></p><p>License: The BSD License (<a href="http://www.opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php">http://www.opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php</a>)</p><p>The snowball stemmers in analysis/common/src/java/net/sf/snowball were developed by Martin Porter and Richard Boulton. The snowball stopword lists in analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/snowball</p><p>were developed by Martin Porter and Richard Boulton. The full snowball package is available from <a href="http://snowball.tartarus.org/">http://snowball.tartarus.org/</a></p><p>The KStem stemmer in analysis/common/src/org/apache/lucene/analysis/en was developed by Bob Krovetz and Sergio Guzman-Lara (CIIR-UMass Amherst) under the BSD-license.</p><p>The Arabic,Persian,Romanian,Bulgarian, Hindi and Bengali analyzers (common) come with a default</p><p>stopword list that is BSD-licensed created by Jacques Savoy. These files reside in:</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ar/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fa/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ro/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/bg/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/hi/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/bn/stopwords.txt</p><p>See <a href="http://members.unine.ch/jacques.savoy/clef/index.html">http://members.unine.ch/jacques.savoy/clef/index.html</a>.</p><p>The German,Spanish,Finnish,French,Hungarian,Italian,Portuguese,Russian and Swedish light stemmers</p><p>(common) are based on BSD-licensed reference implementations created by Jacques Savoy and</p><p>Ljiljana Dolamic. These files reside in:</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/de/GermanLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/de/GermanMinimalStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/es/SpanishLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fi/FinnishLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fr/FrenchLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fr/FrenchMinimalStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/hu/HungarianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/it/ItalianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/pt/PortugueseLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ru/RussianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/sv/SwedishLightStemmer.java</p><p>The Stempel analyzer (stempel) includes BSD-licensed software developed by the Egothor project <a href="http://egothor.sf.net/">http://egothor.sf.net/</a>, created by Leo Galambos, Martin Kvapil, and Edmond Nolan.</p><p>The Polish analyzer (stempel) comes with a default stopword list that is BSD-licensed created by the Carrot2 project. The file resides in stempel/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/pl/stopwords.txt. See <a href="http://project.carrot2.org/license.html">http://project.carrot2.org/license.html</a>.</p><p>The SmartChineseAnalyzer source code (smartcn) was provided by Xiaoping Gao and copyright 2009 by <a href="http://www.imdict.net">www.imdict.net</a>.</p><p>WordBreakTestUnicode_*.java (under modules/analysis/common/src/test/) is derived from Unicode data such as the Unicode Character Database. See <a href="http://unicode.org/copyright.html">http://unicode.org/copyright.html</a> for more details.</p><p>The Morfologik analyzer (morfologik) includes BSD-licensed software developed by Dawid Weiss and Marcin Miłkowski (<a href="http://morfologik.blogspot.com/">http://morfologik.blogspot.com/</a>).</p><p>Morfologik uses data from Polish ispell/myspell dictionary (<a href="http://www.sjp.pl/slownik/en/">http://www.sjp.pl/slownik/en/</a>) licenced on the terms of (inter alia) LGPL and Creative Commons ShareAlike.</p><p>Morfologic includes data from BSD-licensed dictionary of Polish (SGJP) (<a href="http://sgjp.pl/morfeusz/">http://sgjp.pl/morfeusz/</a>)</p><p>Servlet-api.jar and javax.servlet-*.jar are under the CDDL license, the original source code for this can be found at <a href="http://www.eclipse.org/jetty/downloads.php">http://www.eclipse.org/jetty/downloads.php</a></p><p>===========================================================================</p><p>Kuromoji Japanese Morphological Analyzer - Apache Lucene Integration</p><p>===========================================================================</p><p>This software includes a binary and/or source version of data from</p><p>mecab-ko-dic-2.0.3-20170922</p><p>which can be obtained from</p><p><a href="https://bitbucket.org/eunjeon/mecab-ko-dic/downloads/mecab-ko-dic-2.0.3-20170922.tar.gz">https://bitbucket.org/eunjeon/mecab-ko-dic/downloads/mecab-ko-dic-2.0.3-20170922.tar.gz</a></p><p>The floating point precision conversion in NumericUtils.Float16Converter is derived from work by</p><p>Jeroen van der Zijp, granted for use under the Apache license.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="2"><p><span class="ph">Lucene Queries<span> </span> </span> <span class="ph">8.11.3</span></p><p>Apache Lucene</p><p>Copyright 2001-2021 The Apache Software Foundation</p><p>This product includes software developed at The Apache Software Foundation (<a href="http://www.apache.org/">http://www.apache.org/</a>).</p><p>Includes software from other Apache Software Foundation projects, including, but not limited to:</p><p>- Apache Ant</p><p>- Apache Jakarta Regexp</p><p>- Apache Commons</p><p>- Apache Xerces</p><p>ICU4J, (under analysis/icu) is licensed under an MIT styles license and Copyright © 1995-2008 International Business Machines Corporation and others</p><p>Some data files (under analysis/icu/src/data) are derived from Unicode data such as the Unicode Character Database. See <a href="http://www.brics.dk/automaton/">http://www.brics.dk/automaton/</a></p><p>The levenshtein automata tables (under core/src/java/org/apache/lucene/util/automaton) were automatically generated with the moman/finenight FSA library, created by Jean-Philippe Barrette-LaPierre. This library is available under an MIT license, see <a href="http://sites.google.com/site/rrettesite/moman">http://sites.google.com/site/rrettesite/moman</a> and <a href="http://bitbucket.org/jpbarrette/moman/overview/">http://bitbucket.org/jpbarrette/moman/overview/</a></p><p>The class org.apache.lucene.util.WeakIdentityMap was derived from the Apache CXF project and is Apache License 2.0.</p><p>The class org.apache.lucene.util.compress.LZ4 is a Java rewrite of the LZ4 compression library (<a href="https://github.com/lz4/lz4/tree/dev/lib">https://github.com/lz4/lz4/tree/dev/lib</a>) that is licensed under the 2-clause BSD license. (<a href="https://opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php">https://opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php</a>)</p><p>The Google Code Prettify is Apache License 2.0.</p><p>See <a href="http://code.google.com/p/google-code-prettify/">http://code.google.com/p/google-code-prettify/</a></p><p>JUnit (junit-4.10) is licensed under the Common Public License v. 1.0</p><p>See <a href="http://junit.sourceforge.net/cpl-v10.html">http://junit.sourceforge.net/cpl-v10.html</a></p><p>This product includes code (JaspellTernarySearchTrie) from Java Spelling Checkin g Package (jaspell): <a href="http://jaspell.sourceforge.net/">http://jaspell.sourceforge.net/</a></p><p>License: The BSD License (<a href="http://www.opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php">http://www.opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php</a>)</p><p>The snowball stemmers in analysis/common/src/java/net/sf/snowball were developed by Martin Porter and Richard Boulton. The snowball stopword lists in analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/snowball</p><p>were developed by Martin Porter and Richard Boulton. The full snowball package is available from <a href="http://snowball.tartarus.org/">http://snowball.tartarus.org/</a></p><p>The KStem stemmer in analysis/common/src/org/apache/lucene/analysis/en was developed by Bob Krovetz and Sergio Guzman-Lara (CIIR-UMass Amherst) under the BSD-license.</p><p>The Arabic,Persian,Romanian,Bulgarian, Hindi and Bengali analyzers (common) come with a default</p><p>stopword list that is BSD-licensed created by Jacques Savoy. These files reside in:</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ar/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fa/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ro/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/bg/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/hi/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/bn/stopwords.txt</p><p>See <a href="http://members.unine.ch/jacques.savoy/clef/index.html">http://members.unine.ch/jacques.savoy/clef/index.html</a>.</p><p>The German,Spanish,Finnish,French,Hungarian,Italian,Portuguese,Russian and Swedish light stemmers</p><p>(common) are based on BSD-licensed reference implementations created by Jacques Savoy and</p><p>Ljiljana Dolamic. These files reside in:</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/de/GermanLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/de/GermanMinimalStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/es/SpanishLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fi/FinnishLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fr/FrenchLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fr/FrenchMinimalStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/hu/HungarianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/it/ItalianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/pt/PortugueseLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ru/RussianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/sv/SwedishLightStemmer.java</p><p>The Stempel analyzer (stempel) includes BSD-licensed software developed by the Egothor project <a href="http://egothor.sf.net/">http://egothor.sf.net/</a>, created by Leo Galambos, Martin Kvapil, and Edmond Nolan.</p><p>The Polish analyzer (stempel) comes with a default stopword list that is BSD-licensed created by the Carrot2 project. The file resides in stempel/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/pl/stopwords.txt. See <a href="http://project.carrot2.org/license.html">http://project.carrot2.org/license.html</a>.</p><p>The SmartChineseAnalyzer source code (smartcn) was provided by Xiaoping Gao and copyright 2009 by <a href="http://www.imdict.net">www.imdict.net</a>.</p><p>WordBreakTestUnicode_*.java (under modules/analysis/common/src/test/) is derived from Unicode data such as the Unicode Character Database. Apache Lucene Integration</p><p>===========================================================================</p><p>This software includes a binary and/or source version of data from</p><p>mecab-ko-dic-2.0.3-20170922</p><p>which can be obtained from</p><p><a href="https://bitbucket.org/eunjeon/mecab-ko-dic/downloads/mecab-ko-dic-2.0.3-20170922.tar.gz">https://bitbucket.org/eunjeon/mecab-ko-dic/downloads/mecab-ko-dic-2.0.3-20170922.tar.gz</a></p></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="2"><p><span class="ph">Lucene Queryparser<span> </span> </span> <span class="ph">8.11.3</span></p><p>Apache Lucene</p><p>Copyright 2001-2021 The Apache Software Foundation</p><p>This product includes software developed at The Apache Software Foundation (<a href="http://www.apache.org/">http://www.apache.org/</a>).</p><p>Includes software from other Apache Software Foundation projects, including, but not limited to:</p><p>- Apache Ant</p><p>- Apache Jakarta Regexp</p><p>- Apache Commons</p><p>- Apache Xerces</p><p>ICU4J, (under analysis/icu) is licensed under an MIT styles license and Copyright © 1995-2008 International Business Machines Corporation and others</p><p>Some data files (under analysis/icu/src/data) are derived from Unicode data such as the Unicode Character Database. See <a href="http://unicode.org/copyright.html">http://unicode.org/copyright.html</a> for more details.</p><p>Brics Automaton (under core/src/java/org/apache/lucene/util/automaton) is BSD-licensed, created by Anders Møller. See <a href="http://www.brics.dk/automaton/">http://www.brics.dk/automaton/</a></p><p>The levenshtein automata tables (under core/src/java/org/apache/lucene/util/automaton) were automatically generated with the moman/finenight FSA library, created by Jean-Philippe Barrette-LaPierre. This library is available under an MIT license, see <a href="http://sites.google.com/site/rrettesite/moman">http://sites.google.com/site/rrettesite/moman</a> and <a href="http://bitbucket.org/jpbarrette/moman/overview/">http://bitbucket.org/jpbarrette/moman/overview/</a></p><p>The class org.apache.lucene.util.WeakIdentityMap was derived from the Apache CXF project and is Apache License 2.0.</p><p>The class org.apache.lucene.util.compress.LZ4 is a Java rewrite of the LZ4 compression library (<a href="https://github.com/lz4/lz4/tree/dev/lib">https://github.com/lz4/lz4/tree/dev/lib</a>) that is licensed under the 2-clause BSD license. (<a href="https://opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php">https://opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php</a>)</p><p>The Google Code Prettify is Apache License 2.0.</p><p>See <a href="http://code.google.com/p/google-code-prettify/">http://code.google.com/p/google-code-prettify/</a></p><p>JUnit (junit-4.10) is licensed under the Common Public License v. 1.0</p><p>See <a href="http://junit.sourceforge.net/cpl-v10.html">http://junit.sourceforge.net/cpl-v10.html</a></p><p>This product includes code (JaspellTernarySearchTrie) from Java Spelling Checkin g Package (jaspell): <a href="http://jaspell.sourceforge.net/">http://jaspell.sourceforge.net/</a></p><p>License: The BSD License (<a href="http://www.opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php">http://www.opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php</a>)</p><p>The snowball stemmers in analysis/common/src/java/net/sf/snowball were developed by Martin Porter and Richard Boulton. The snowball stopword lists in analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/snowball</p><p>were developed by Martin Porter and Richard Boulton. The full snowball package is available from <a href="http://snowball.tartarus.org/">http://snowball.tartarus.org/</a></p><p>The KStem stemmer in analysis/common/src/org/apache/lucene/analysis/en was developed by Bob Krovetz and Sergio Guzman-Lara (CIIR-UMass Amherst) under the BSD-license.</p><p>The Arabic,Persian,Romanian,Bulgarian, Hindi and Bengali analyzers (common) come with a default</p><p>stopword list that is BSD-licensed created by Jacques Savoy. These files reside in:</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ar/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fa/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ro/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/bg/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/hi/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/bn/stopwords.txt</p><p>See <a href="http://members.unine.ch/jacques.savoy/clef/index.html">http://members.unine.ch/jacques.savoy/clef/index.html</a>.</p><p>The German,Spanish,Finnish,French,Hungarian,Italian,Portuguese,Russian and Swedish light stemmers</p><p>(common) are based on BSD-licensed reference implementations created by Jacques Savoy and</p><p>Ljiljana Dolamic. These files reside in:</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/de/GermanLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/de/GermanMinimalStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/es/SpanishLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fi/FinnishLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fr/FrenchLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fr/FrenchMinimalStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/hu/HungarianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/it/ItalianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/pt/PortugueseLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ru/RussianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/sv/SwedishLightStemmer.java</p><p>The Stempel analyzer (stempel) includes BSD-licensed software developed by the Egothor project <a href="http://egothor.sf.net/">http://egothor.sf.net/</a>, created by Leo Galambos, Martin Kvapil, and Edmond Nolan.</p><p>The Polish analyzer (stempel) comes with a default stopword list that is BSD-licensed created by the Carrot2 project. The file resides in stempel/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/pl/stopwords.txt. See <a href="http://project.carrot2.org/license.html">http://project.carrot2.org/license.html</a>.</p><p>The SmartChineseAnalyzer source code (smartcn) was provided by Xiaoping Gao and copyright 2009 by <a href="http://www.imdict.net">www.imdict.net</a>.</p><p>WordBreakTestUnicode_*.java (under modules/analysis/common/src/test/) is derived from Unicode data such as the Unicode Character Database. See <a href="http://unicode.org/copyright.html">http://unicode.org/copyright.html</a> for more details.</p><p>The Morfologik analyzer (morfologik) includes BSD-licensed software developed by Dawid Weiss and Marcin Miłkowski (<a href="http://morfologik.blogspot.com/">http://morfologik.blogspot.com/</a>).</p><p>Morfologik uses data from Polish ispell/myspell dictionary (<a href="http://www.sjp.pl/slownik/en/">http://www.sjp.pl/slownik/en/</a>) licenced on the terms of (inter alia) LGPL and Creative Commons ShareAlike.</p><p>Morfologic includes data from BSD-licensed dictionary of Polish (SGJP) (<a href="http://sgjp.pl/morfeusz/">http://sgjp.pl/morfeusz/</a>)</p><p>Servlet-api.jar and javax.servlet-*.jar are under the CDDL license, the original source code for this can be found at <a href="http://www.eclipse.org/jetty/downloads.php">http://www.eclipse.org/jetty/downloads.php</a></p><p>===========================================================================</p><p>Kuromoji Japanese Morphological Analyzer - Apache Lucene Integration</p><p>===========================================================================</p><p>This software includes a binary and/or source version of data from</p><p>mecab-ipadic-2.7.0-20070801</p><p>which can be obtained from</p><p><a href="http://atilika.com/releases/mecab-ipadic/mecab-ipadic-2.7.0-20070801.tar.gz">http://atilika.com/releases/mecab-ipadic/mecab-ipadic-2.7.0-20070801.tar.gz</a></p><p>or</p><p><a href="http://jaist.dl.sourceforge.net/project/mecab/mecab-ipadic/2.7.0-20070801/mecab-ipadic-2.7.0-20070801.tar.gz">http://jaist.dl.sourceforge.net/project/mecab/mecab-ipadic/2.7.0-20070801/mecab-ipadic-2.7.0-20070801.tar.gz</a></p><p>===========================================================================</p><p>mecab-ipadic-2.7.0-20070801 Notice</p><p>===========================================================================</p><p>Nara Institute of Science and Technology (NAIST), the copyright holders, disclaims all warranties with regard to this software, including all implied warranties of merchantability and fitness, in no event shall NAIST be liable for any special, indirect or consequential damages or any damages whatsoever resulting from loss of use, data or profits, whether in an action of contract, negligence or other tortuous action, arising out of or in connection with the use or performance of this software.</p><p>A large portion of the dictionary entries originate from ICOT Free Software. This library is available under an MIT license, see <a href="http://sites.google.com/site/rrettesite/moman">http://sites.google.com/site/rrettesite/moman</a> and <a href="http://bitbucket.org/jpbarrette/moman/overview/">http://bitbucket.org/jpbarrette/moman/overview/</a></p><p>The class org.apache.lucene.util.WeakIdentityMap was derived from the Apache CXF project and is Apache License 2.0.</p><p>The class org.apache.lucene.util.compress.LZ4 is a Java rewrite of the LZ4 compression library (<a href="https://github.com/lz4/lz4/tree/dev/lib">https://github.com/lz4/lz4/tree/dev/lib</a>) that is licensed under the 2-clause BSD license. (<a href="https://opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php">https://opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php</a>)</p><p>The Google Code Prettify is Apache License 2.0.</p><p>See <a href="http://code.google.com/p/google-code-prettify/">http://code.google.com/p/google-code-prettify/</a></p><p>JUnit (junit-4.10) is licensed under the Common Public License v. 1.0</p><p>See <a href="http://junit.sourceforge.net/cpl-v10.html">http://junit.sourceforge.net/cpl-v10.html</a></p><p>This product includes code (JaspellTernarySearchTrie) from Java Spelling Checkin g Package (jaspell): <a href="http://jaspell.sourceforge.net/">http://jaspell.sourceforge.net/</a></p><p>License: The BSD License (<a href="http://www.opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php">http://www.opensource.org/licenses/bsd-license.php</a>)</p><p>The snowball stemmers in analysis/common/src/java/net/sf/snowball were developed by Martin Porter and Richard Boulton. The snowball stopword lists in analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/snowball</p><p>were developed by Martin Porter and Richard Boulton. The full snowball package is available from <a href="http://snowball.tartarus.org/">http://snowball.tartarus.org/</a></p><p>The KStem stemmer in analysis/common/src/org/apache/lucene/analysis/en was developed by Bob Krovetz and Sergio Guzman-Lara (CIIR-UMass Amherst) under the BSD-license.</p><p>The Arabic,Persian,Romanian,Bulgarian, Hindi and Bengali analyzers (common) come with a default</p><p>stopword list that is BSD-licensed created by Jacques Savoy. These files reside in:</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ar/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fa/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ro/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/bg/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/hi/stopwords.txt,</p><p>analysis/common/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/bn/stopwords.txt</p><p>See <a href="http://members.unine.ch/jacques.savoy/clef/index.html">http://members.unine.ch/jacques.savoy/clef/index.html</a>.</p><p>The German,Spanish,Finnish,French,Hungarian,Italian,Portuguese,Russian and Swedish light stemmers</p><p>(common) are based on BSD-licensed reference implementations created by Jacques Savoy and</p><p>Ljiljana Dolamic. These files reside in:</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/de/GermanLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/de/GermanMinimalStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/es/SpanishLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fi/FinnishLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fr/FrenchLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/fr/FrenchMinimalStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/hu/HungarianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/it/ItalianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/pt/PortugueseLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/ru/RussianLightStemmer.java</p><p>analysis/common/src/java/org/apache/lucene/analysis/sv/SwedishLightStemmer.java</p><p>The Stempel analyzer (stempel) includes BSD-licensed software developed by the Egothor project <a href="http://egothor.sf.net/">http://egothor.sf.net/</a>, created by Leo Galambos, Martin Kvapil, and Edmond Nolan.</p><p>The Polish analyzer (stempel) comes with a default stopword list that is BSD-licensed created by the Carrot2 project. The file resides in stempel/src/resources/org/apache/lucene/analysis/pl/stopwords.txt. See <a href="http://project.carrot2.org/license.html">http://project.carrot2.org/license.html</a>.</p><p>The SmartChineseAnalyzer source code (smartcn) was provided by Xiaoping Gao and copyright 2009 by <a href="http://www.imdict.net">www.imdict.net</a>.</p><p>WordBreakTestUnicode_*.java (under modules/analysis/common/src/test/) is derived from Unicode data such as the Unicode Character Database. See <a href="http://unicode.org/copyright.html">http://unicode.org/copyright.html</a> for more details.</p><p>The Morfologik analyzer (morfologik) includes BSD-licensed software developed by Dawid Weiss and Marcin Miłkowski (<a href="http://morfologik.blogspot.com/">http://morfologik.blogspot.com/</a>).</p><p>Morfologik uses data from Polish ispell/myspell dictionary (<a href="http://www.sjp.pl/slownik/en/">http://www.sjp.pl/slownik/en/</a>) licenced on the terms of (inter alia) LGPL and Creative Commons ShareAlike.</p><p>Morfologic includes data from BSD-licensed dictionary of Polish (SGJP) (<a href="http://sgjp.pl/morfeusz/">http://sgjp.pl/morfeusz/</a>)</p><p>Servlet-api.jar and javax.servlet-*.jar are under the CDDL license, the original source code for this can be found at <a href="http://www.eclipse.org/jetty/downloads.php">http://www.eclipse.org/jetty/downloads.php</a></p><p>===========================================================================</p><p>Kuromoji Japanese Morphological Analyzer - The Purpose of this Agreement.</p><p>This Agreement sets forth the terms under which I make certain copyright and patent rights available to you for your implementation of the Specification.</p><p>Any other capitalized terms not specifically defined herein have the same meaning as those terms have in the &quot;W3C Patent Policy&quot;, and if not defined there, in the &quot;W3C Process Document&quot;.</p><p>2. Copyrights.</p><p>2.1. Copyright Grant. I grant to you a perpetual (for the duration of the applicable copyright), worldwide, non-exclusive, no-charge, royalty-free, copyright license, without any obligation for accounting to me, to reproduce, prepare derivative works of, publicly display, publicly perform, sublicense, distribute, and implement the Specification to the full extent of my copyright interest in the Specification.</p><p>2.2. Attribution. As a condition of the copyright grant, you must include an attribution to the Specification in any derivative work you make based on the Specification. That attribution must include, at minimum, the Specification name and version number.</p><p>3. Patents.</p><p>3.1. Patent Licensing Commitment. I agree to license my Essential Claims under the W3C Community RF Licensing Requirements. This requirement includes Essential Claims that I own and any that I have the right to license without obligation of payment or other consideration to an unrelated third party. W3C Community RF Licensing Requirements obligations made concerning the Specification and described in this policy are binding on me for the life of the patents in question and encumber the patents containing Essential Claims, regardless of changes in participation status or W3C Membership. I also agree to license my Essential Claims under the W3C Community RF Licensing Requirements in derivative works of the Specification so long as all normative portions of the Specification are maintained and that this licensing commitment does not extend to any portion of the derivative work that was not included in the Specification.</p><p>3.2. Optional, Additional Patent Grant. In addition to the provisions of Section 3.1, I may also, at my option, make certain intellectual property rights infringed by implementations of the Specification, including Essential Claims, available by providing those terms via the W3C Web site.</p><p>4. No Other Rights. Except as specifically set forth in this Agreement, no other express or implied patent, trademark, copyright, or other property rights are granted under this Agreement, including by implication, waiver, or estoppel.</p><p>5. Antitrust Compliance. I acknowledge that I may compete with other participants, that I am under no obligation to implement the Specification, that each participant is free to develop competing technologies and standards, and that each party is free to license its patent rights to third parties, including for the purpose of enabling competing technologies and standards.</p><p>6. Non-Circumvention. I agree that I will not intentionally take or willfully assist any third party to take any action for the purpose of circumventing my obligations under this Agreement.</p><p>7. Transition to W3C Recommendation Track. The Specification developed by the Project may transition to the W3C Recommendation Track. The W3C Team is responsible for notifying me that a Corresponding Working Group has been chartered. I have no obligation to join the Corresponding Working Group. If the Specification developed by the Project transitions to the W3C Recommendation Track, the following terms apply:</p><p>7.1. If I join the Corresponding Working Group. If I join the Corresponding Working Group, I will be subject to all W3C rules, obligations, licensing commitments, and policies that govern that Corresponding Working Group.</p><p>7.2. If I Do Not Join the Corresponding Working Group.</p><p>7.2.1. Licensing Obligations to Resulting Specification. If I do not join the Corresponding Working Group, I agree to offer patent licenses according to the W3C Royalty-Free licensing requirements described in Section 5 of the W3C Patent Policy for the portions of the Specification included in the resulting Recommendation. This licensing commitment does not extend to any portion of an implementation of the Recommendation that was not included in the Specification. This licensing commitment may not be revoked but may be modified through the exclusion process defined in Section 4 of the W3C Patent Policy. I am not required to join the Corresponding Working Group to exclude patents from the W3C Royalty-Free licensing commitment, but must otherwise follow the normal exclusion procedures defined by the W3C Patent Policy. The W3C Team will notify me of any Call for Exclusion in the Corresponding Working Group as set forth in Section 4.5 of the W3C Patent Policy.</p><p>7.2.2. No Disclosure Obligation. If I do not join the Corresponding Working Group, I have no patent disclosure obligations outside of those set forth in Section 6 of the W3C Patent Policy.</p><p>8. Conflict of Interest. I will disclose significant relationships when those relationships might reasonably be perceived as creating a conflict of interest with my role. I will notify W3C of any change in my affiliation using W3C-provided mechanisms.</p><p>9. Representations, Warranties and Disclaimers. I represent and warrant that I am legally entitled to grant the rights and promises set forth in this Agreement. IN ALL OTHER RESPECTS THE SPECIFICATION IS PROVIDED “AS IS.” The entire risk as to implementing or otherwise using the Specification is assumed by the implementer and user. Except as stated herein, I expressly disclaim any warranties (express, implied, or otherwise), including implied warranties of merchantability, non-infringement, fitness for a particular purpose, or title, related to the Specification. IN NO EVENT WILL ANY PARTY BE LIABLE TO ANY OTHER PARTY FOR LOST PROFITS OR ANY FORM OF INDIRECT, SPECIAL, INCIDENTAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES OF ANY CHARACTER FROM ANY CAUSES OF ACTION OF ANY KIND WITH RESPECT TO THIS AGREEMENT, WHETHER BASED ON BREACH OF CONTRACT, TORT (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE), OR OTHERWISE, AND WHETHER OR NOT THE OTHER PARTY HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGE. All of my obligations under Section 3 regarding the transfer, successors in interest, or assignment of Granted Claims will be satisfied if I notify the transferee or assignee of any patent that I know contains Granted Claims of the obligations under Section 3. Nothing in this Agreement requires me to undertake a patent search.</p><p>10. Definitions.</p><p>10.1. Agreement. “Agreement” means this W3C Community Final Specification Agreement.</p><p>10.2. Corresponding Working Group. “Corresponding Working Group” is a W3C Working Group that is chartered to develop a Recommendation, as defined in the W3C Process Document, that takes the Specification as an input.</p><p>10.3. Essential Claims. “Essential Claims” shall mean all claims in any patent or patent application in any jurisdiction in the world that would necessarily be infringed by implementation of the Specification. A claim is necessarily infringed hereunder only when it is not possible to avoid infringing it because there is no non-infringing alternative for implementing the normative portions of the Specification. Existence of a non-infringing alternative shall be judged based on the state of the art at the time of the publication of the Specification. The following are expressly excluded from and shall not be deemed to constitute Essential Claims:</p><p>10.3.1. any claims other than as set forth above even if contained in the same patent as Essential Claims; and</p><p>10.3.2. claims which would be infringed only by:</p><p>portions of an implementation that are not specified in the normative portions of the Specification, or</p><p>enabling technologies that may be necessary to make or use any product or portion thereof that complies with the Specification and are not themselves expressly set forth in the Specification (e.g., semiconductor manufacturing technology, compiler technology, object-oriented technology, basic operating system technology, and the like); or</p><p>the implementation of technology developed elsewhere and merely incorporated by reference in the body of the Specification.</p><p>10.3.3. design patents and design registrations.</p><p>For purposes of this definition, the normative portions of the Specification shall be deemed to include only architectural and interoperability requirements. Optional features in the RFC 2119 sense are considered normative unless they are specifically identified as informative. Implementation examples or any other material that merely illustrate the requirements of the Specification are informative, rather than normative.</p><p>10.4. I, Me, or My. “I,” “me,” or “my” refers to the signatory.</p><p>10.5 Project. “Project” means the W3C Community Group or Business Group for which I executed this Agreement.</p><p>10.6. Specification. “Specification” means the Specification identified by the Project as the target of this agreement in a call for Final Specification Commitments. W3C shall provide the authoritative mechanisms for the identification of this Specification.</p><p>10.7. W3C Community RF Licensing Requirements. “W3C Community RF Licensing Requirements” license shall mean a non-assignable, non-sublicensable license to make, have made, use, sell, have sold, offer to sell, import, and distribute and dispose of implementations of the Specification that:</p><p>10.7.1. shall be available to all, worldwide, whether or not they are W3C Members;</p><p>10.7.2. shall extend to all Essential Claims owned or controlled by me;</p><p>10.7.3. may be limited to implementations of the Specification, and to what is required by the Specification;</p><p>10.7.4. may be conditioned on a grant of a reciprocal RF license (as defined in this policy) to all Essential Claims owned or controlled by the licensee. The RF license conforming to the requirements in this policy shall be made available by the licensor as long as the Specification is in effect. That attribution must include, at minimum, the Specification name and version number.</p><p>3. Patents.</p><p>3.1. Patent Licensing Commitment. I agree to license my Essential Claims under the W3C Community RF Licensing Requirements. This requirement includes Essential Claims that I own and any that I have the right to license without obligation of payment or other consideration to an unrelated third party. I also agree to license my Essential Claims under the W3C Community RF Licensing Requirements in derivative works of the Specification so long as all normative portions of the Specification are maintained and that this licensing commitment does not extend to any portion of the derivative work that was not included in the Specification.</p><p>3.2. Optional, Additional Patent Grant. No Other Rights. Except as specifically set forth in this Agreement, no other express or implied patent, trademark, copyright, or other property rights are granted under this Agreement, including by implication, waiver, or estoppel.</p><p>5. Antitrust Compliance. I acknowledge that I may compete with other participants, that I am under no obligation to implement the Specification, that each participant is free to develop competing technologies and standards, and that each party is free to license its patent rights to third parties, including for the purpose of enabling competing technologies and standards.</p><p>6. Non-Circumvention. I agree that I will not intentionally take or willfully assist any third party to take any action for the purpose of circumventing my obligations under this Agreement.</p><p>7. Transition to W3C Recommendation Track. The Specification developed by the Project may transition to the W3C Recommendation Track. The W3C Team is responsible for notifying me that a Corresponding Working Group has been chartered. I have no obligation to join the Corresponding Working Group. If the Specification developed by the Project transitions to the W3C Recommendation Track, the following terms apply:</p><p>7.1. If I join the Corresponding Working Group. If I join the Corresponding Working Group, I will be subject to all W3C rules, obligations, licensing commitments, and policies that govern that Corresponding Working Group.</p><p>7.2. If I Do Not Join the Corresponding Working Group.</p><p>7.2.1. Licensing Obligations to Resulting Specification. If I do not join the Corresponding Working Group, I agree to offer patent licenses according to the W3C Royalty-Free licensing requirements described in Section 5 of the W3C Patent Policy for the portions of the Specification included in the resulting Recommendation. This licensing commitment does not extend to any portion of an implementation of the Recommendation that was not included in the Specification. This licensing commitment may not be revoked but may be modified through the exclusion process defined in Section 4 of the W3C Patent Policy. I am not required to join the Corresponding Working Group to exclude patents from the W3C Royalty-Free licensing commitment, but must otherwise follow the normal exclusion procedures defined by the W3C Patent Policy. The W3C Team will notify me of any Call for Exclusion in the Corresponding Working Group as set forth in Section 4.5 of the W3C Patent Policy.</p><p>7.2.2. No Disclosure Obligation. If I do not join the Corresponding Working Group, I have no patent disclosure obligations outside of those set forth in Section 6 of the W3C Patent Policy.</p><p>8. Conflict of Interest. I will disclose significant relationships when those relationships might reasonably be perceived as creating a conflict of interest with my role. I will notify W3C of any change in my affiliation using W3C-provided mechanisms.</p><p>9. Representations, Warranties and Disclaimers. All of my obligations under Section 3 regarding the transfer, successors in interest, or assignment of Granted Claims will be satisfied if I notify the transferee or assignee of any patent that I know contains Granted Claims of the obligations under Section 3. Nothing in this Agreement requires me to undertake a patent search.</p><p>10. Definitions.</p><p>10.1. Agreement. “Agreement” means this W3C Community Final Specification Agreement.</p><p>10.2. Corresponding Working Group. “Corresponding Working Group” is a W3C Working Group that is chartered to develop a Recommendation, as defined in the W3C Process Document, that takes the Specification as an input.</p><p>10.3. Essential Claims. “Essential Claims” shall mean all claims in any patent or patent application in any jurisdiction in the world that would necessarily be infringed by implementation of the Specification. A claim is necessarily infringed hereunder only when it is not possible to avoid infringing it because there is no non-infringing alternative for implementing the normative portions of the Specification. Existence of a non-infringing alternative shall be judged based on the state of the art at the time of the publication of the Specification. The following are expressly excluded from and shall not be deemed to constitute Essential Claims:</p><p>10.3.1. any claims other than as set forth above even if contained in the same patent as Essential Claims; and</p><p>10.3.2. claims which would be infringed only by:</p><p>portions of an implementation that are not specified in the normative portions of the Specification, or</p><p>enabling technologies that may be necessary to make or use any product or portion thereof that complies with the Specification and are not themselves expressly set forth in the Specification (e.g., semiconductor manufacturing technology, compiler technology, object-oriented technology, basic operating system technology, and the like); or</p><p>the implementation of technology developed elsewhere and merely incorporated by reference in the body of the Specification.</p><p>10.3.3. design patents and design registrations.</p><p>For purposes of this definition, the normative portions of the Specification shall be deemed to include only architectural and interoperability requirements. Optional features in the RFC 2119 sense are considered normative unless they are specifically identified as informative. Implementation examples or any other material that merely illustrate the requirements of the Specification are informative, rather than normative.</p><p>10.4. I, Me, or My. “I,” “me,” or “my” refers to the signatory.</p><p>10.5 Project. “Project” means the W3C Community Group or Business Group for which I executed this Agreement.</p><p>10.6. Specification. “Specification” means the Specification identified by the Project as the target of this agreement in a call for Final Specification Commitments. Corresponding Working Group. “Corresponding Working Group” is a W3C Working Group that is chartered to develop a Recommendation, as defined in the W3C Process Document, that takes the Specification as an input.</p><p>10.3. Essential Claims. “Essential Claims” shall mean all claims in any patent or patent application in any jurisdiction in the world that would necessarily be infringed by implementation of the Specification. A claim is necessarily infringed hereunder only when it is not possible to avoid infringing it because there is no non-infringing alternative for implementing the normative portions of the Specification. Existence of a non-infringing alternative shall be judged based on the state of the art at the time of the publication of the Specification. The following are expressly excluded from and shall not be deemed to constitute Essential Claims:</p><p>10.3.1. any claims other than as set forth above even if contained in the same patent as Essential Claims; and</p><p>10.3.2. claims which would be infringed only by:</p><p>portions of an implementation that are not specified in the normative portions of the Specification, or</p><p>enabling technologies that may be necessary to make or use any product or portion thereof that complies with the Specification and are not themselves expressly set forth in the Specification (e.g., semiconductor manufacturing technology, compiler technology, object-oriented technology, basic operating system technology, and the like); or</p><p>the implementation of technology developed elsewhere and merely incorporated by reference in the body of the Specification.</p><p>10.3.3. design patents and design registrations.</p><p>For purposes of this definition, the normative portions of the Specification shall be deemed to include only architectural and interoperability requirements. Optional features in the RFC 2119 sense are considered normative unless they are specifically identified as informative. Implementation examples or any other material that merely illustrate the requirements of the Specification are informative, rather than normative.</p><p>10.4. I, Me, or My. “I,” “me,” or “my” refers to the signatory.</p><p>10.5 Project. “Project” means the W3C Community Group or Business Group for which I executed this Agreement.</p><p>10.6. Specification. “Specification” means the Specification identified by the Project as the target of this agreement in a call for Final Specification Commitments. It is numbered 2 because it goes with version 2 of the ordinary GPL.]</p><p>Preamble</p><p>The licenses for most software are designed to take away your freedom to share and change it.
